name: Lake Coeur d'Alene
description:
Lake Coeur d'Alene is one of the most beautiful lakes in the American West, a 25-mile-long glacial lake in the Idaho Panhandle. The crystal-clear water and forested shoreline create a stunning fishing environment.

Chinook salmon are the premier target, with the lake's landlocked population producing fish exceeding 20 pounds. Cutthroat trout provide action near the surface, while smallmouth and largemouth bass inhabit the warmer shallows and docks. Northern pike have become established and offer trophy-size catches. Multiple public launches provide access around the lake.

name: Payette Lake
description:
Payette Lake is a deep, cold mountain lake at 5,020 feet elevation near the charming resort town of McCall. The lake occupies a glacial basin and offers excellent cold-water fishing in a pristine setting.

Lake trout (mackinaw) are the trophy fish, with specimens exceeding 30 pounds pulled from the depths. Trolling for rainbow trout and kokanee salmon is productive during summer months. The lake's public beach and boat ramp in McCall provide the primary access, with additional launches at the north end.

name: Priest Lake
description:
Priest Lake is a remote, pristine mountain lake in northern Idaho's Selkirk Mountains, known for its remarkably clear water and wilderness character. The lake is connected to Upper Priest Lake by a 2-mile thoroughfare.

Lake trout (mackinaw) grow to impressive sizes in the deep, cold water and are the primary trophy target. Native Westslope cutthroat trout provide surface action, especially during insect hatches. The remote location keeps fishing pressure moderate despite the lake's excellent fishing. Several Forest Service and state launches provide access.

name: Dworshak Reservoir
description:
Dworshak Reservoir is one of the deepest reservoirs in the western United States, formed by the tallest straight-axis dam in North America on the North Fork of the Clearwater River. The lake stretches 54 miles through remote Idaho wilderness.

Smallmouth bass fishing is world-class, with the warm upper layer producing aggressive bronzebacks along rocky banks. Kokanee salmon thrive in the cold, deep water and are a popular trolling target. The reservoir's remote arms offer solitude rarely found on waters this size. Three developed launches provide access.

name: Salmon River
description:
The Salmon River, known as the River of No Return, is Idaho's most famous waterway, flowing 425 miles through some of the most rugged wilderness in the lower 48 states. The river supports runs of wild steelhead and Chinook salmon that travel 900 miles from the Pacific.

Steelhead fishing from October through April draws dedicated anglers to brave cold conditions for these powerful ocean-run trout. Summer Chinook salmon runs provide exciting fishing in the upper river near Stanley. Resident cutthroat and rainbow trout offer year-round fishing. Boat launches at key access points allow drift boat trips through spectacular canyon scenery.

name: Snake River (ID)
description:
The Snake River crosses southern Idaho for over 500 miles, providing diverse fishing from cold spring-fed reaches near Hagerman to warm-water sections near the Boise area. The river is one of the most productive trout streams in the West.

The Hagerman Reach between Thousand Springs and King Hill produces trophy brown and rainbow trout in the crystal-clear, spring-fed water. Sturgeon fishing in Hells Canyon offers the chance to hook fish exceeding 8 feet. Smallmouth bass thrive in the warmer sections. Multiple public access points and ramps are available throughout the river corridor.

name: Lucky Peak Reservoir
description:
Lucky Peak Reservoir is the closest major reservoir to Boise, located just 15 miles southeast of downtown in the Boise River canyon. The Army Corps of Engineers dam creates a narrow, deep lake ideal for both fishing and recreation.

Kokanee salmon fishing draws the largest crowds, with summer trolling producing limits of these landlocked sockeye. Rainbow trout grow quickly in the cold, clear water. The spring arm provides warmer water for smallmouth bass. Spring Shores Marina provides the primary boat launch with excellent facilities.

name: Anderson Ranch Reservoir
description:
Anderson Ranch Reservoir is a remote reservoir on the South Fork of the Boise River, offering outstanding fishing in a beautiful mountain setting at 4,196 feet elevation. The lake's isolation keeps fishing pressure relatively light.

Rainbow trout are the primary target, with fish regularly reaching 3-5 pounds in the cold, nutrient-rich water. Kokanee salmon provide excellent trolling from late spring through summer. Bull trout, a threatened species, are present and must be released. Two boat ramps provide access, with the Pine boat ramp being the most developed.
